steps: By the Universal Coefficient Theorem with $\mathbb{Z}/2$ coefficients: $H^k(\mathbb{R}P^n;\,\mathbb{Z}/2) \cong \mathbb{Z}/2$ for all $0 \leq k \leq n$.
Let $\alpha \in H^1(\mathbb{R}P^n;\,\mathbb{Z}/2) \cong \mathbb{Z}/2$ be the unique non-zero element (Stiefel–Whitney class $w_1$ of the tautological bundle).
Over $\mathbb{Z}/2$, graded commutativity $\alpha^2 = (-1)^{1\cdot 1}\alpha^2 = -\alpha^2 = \alpha^2$ is trivially satisfied, so $\alpha^2$ is not forced to be zero.
One computes (e.g.\ via cellular cochains or the Leray–Hirsch theorem) that $\alpha^k \in H^k(\mathbb{R}P^n;\,\mathbb{Z}/2)$ is the unique nonzero element for each $0 \leq k \leq n$.
